entry.title: Where can I start learning the all about the ey products of SAP Intelligent Asset Management (IAM)?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Discovering Business Processes in SAP Intelligent Asset Management learning journey

Overview
This learning journey will introduce you to the business processes and capabilities of SAP Intelligent Asset Management which supports full digital representation of connected assets along their lifecycle delivering an embedded, collaborative and real-time set of next generation processes and systems.

Learning objectives
After completing this learning journey, Business Users and Consultants will be able to ​identify the key products of SAP Intelligent Asset Management (IAM), describe how each IAM product contributes to the next-generation asset management and discover how the IAM solution can enhance your maintenance strategy.

Prerequisites

  • Discover SAP S/4HANA Asset Management and SAP S/4HANA Service

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Embedding Generative AI into SAP S/4HANA: A Holistic Approach to Intelligent Business Operations
entry.content:
In the fast-paced world of enterprise technology, Generative AI is making waves by offering capabilities far beyond traditional AI. In SAP S/4HANA, this evolution is driving a new era of transactional intelligence where AI not only generates text or automates workflows but actively engages with real-time data and enterprise processes to enhance business operations.

In this post, we’ll explore how Generative AI is embedded into SAP S/4HANA, transforming how businesses interact with their core systems, from automating content creation to making informed transactional decisions. This is based on the latest advancements highlighted by SAP’s own Generative AI solution concepts and expanding use cases​.

Generative AI: Moving from Content to Transactions

Generative AI traditionally started as a tool for text generation, handling tasks like content creation, summarization, or code generation. However, in the context of SAP, Generative AI’s role has expanded dramatically.

We now see GenAI being applied to a wider range of processes:

  1. Text -> Code -> Workflow -> Transactional Data: Generative AI in SAP now supports generating not just textual outputs but handling workflows and interacting with transactional data. Whether it's automating invoice generation, managing inventory, or creating product orders, GenAI can handle the entire flow autonomously, offering solutions to business challenges as they arise​.
  2. Prompting Large Language Models (LLMs) for Enhanced Business Interaction:
    • LLM Retrieval via Fine-Tuning and RAG: Through Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), businesses can now send SAP data into LLMs via HANA vector databases to receive precise responses based on real-time transactional information. Imagine asking the system, "Can this sales order be fulfilled by the end of the month?" and getting a direct, data-backed response​.
    • Document Processing through OCR and LLMs: GenAI can take unstructured data, such as scanned PDFs, and transform it into structured formats like JSON, automating document management processes like invoice extraction or contract analysis​.

These advancements enable non-technical users to interact with SAP’s intelligent core using natural language prompts, making complex business operations more intuitive and efficient.

Expanding LLM Capabilities for SAP S/4HANA

One of the most exciting advancements is the ability to extend LLMs to act as intelligent agents within the SAP environment. LLMs are no longer passive tools but can now plan, execute, and optimize business processes autonomously.

Key innovations include:

  1. LLM-Driven SAP API Integration: Generative AI is integrated with SAP APIs, allowing LLMs to autonomously retrieve API data, plan business actions (such as initiating a production order), and even execute these actions without human intervention​.
    • Example: The LLM accesses the SAP API_CORE, retrieves available APIs, and autonomously configures business processes such as setting up an order, managing inventory, or scheduling deliveries. This level of intelligent automation reduces manual intervention and accelerates business decisions.
  2. Four-Tier LLM Structure for Enhanced Enterprise Automation:
    • General-purpose LLMs: Handle generic language and data tasks.
    • Domain-specific LLMs: Fine-tuned with SAP data, these models can understand complex enterprise data and processes.
    • Cross-task LLMs: Perform more specialized functions like job posting creation, purchase order generation, and workflow automation.
    • Task-specific LLMs: Fully customized for specific business tasks, such as generating API calls for unique operational needs​(Embedding Generative AI…).

This hierarchical model structure offers both broad and specialized AI capabilities, empowering businesses to customize their AI solutions for optimal performance. A detailed blog is available on LAM (Large Action Model) powered agents for enterprise process automation to execute a bunch of tasks.

Building SAP’s Own Generative AI Foundation Models

SAP is moving toward building its own Generative AI foundation models, specifically designed to handle the unique challenges of enterprise environments. These models are not just about handling text or data—they integrate business process context, industry-specific knowledge, and transactional intelligence.

SAP’s own foundation models are trained on structured data, such as financial transactions, sales orders, and manufacturing records, as well as tabular and graph-based data. This integration allows businesses to query real-time transactional data for more accurate, predictive decision-making.

For example:

  • Predictive Transactional Intelligence: Imagine asking the system, “Will this product shipment arrive on time based on current production schedules?” SAP’s GenAI model, trained on past transactional data and current operational insights, will deliver a reliable prediction​.

SAP’s approach focuses on integrating these foundation models with business process metadata, creating a knowledge graph that continuously improves AI accuracy and relevance in real-time business contexts.

Solution Architecture: Embedding Generative AI in SAP S/4HANA

SAP’s Generative AI solution architecture is built on a scalable and adaptable framework. The architecture leverages SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) to provide advanced capabilities in prompt engineering, embedding lookup, and model fine-tuning, all of which are key to customizing the AI to the needs of each enterprise.

Key components of the architecture include:

  1. Joule Integration: Joule is a natural language interface that helps users retrieve information or execute tasks through Conversational AI. The assistant interacts with the Generative AI models to provide real-time business insights​. A detailed blog will be available soon on Joule implementation.
  2. Advanced Prompting: For applications requiring complex prompt engineering, SAP’s BTP provides capabilities such as embedding domain-specific knowledge or leveraging vector search engines to improve the relevance of AI outputs. This ensures that the prompts provided to the LLMs are fine-tuned to yield the most accurate responses​.

Model Retraining for Custom Business Needs: SAP’s architecture supports model retraining using specific enterprise data. This allows businesses to continuously improve their AI models to match evolving operational requirements​.

Conclusion: Generative AI and the Future of SAP S/4HANA

The integration of Generative AI into SAP S/4HANA marks a turning point in the evolution of enterprise AI. With SAP building its own foundation models, capable of handling not just content but transactional and predictive business intelligence, the potential for businesses to automate, optimize, and innovate has never been greater.

From intelligent document processing to real-time decision-making and workflow automation, Generative AI is not only enhancing how enterprises operate but is paving the way for the future of the intelligent enterprise.

If you haven’t explored the potential of embedding Generative AI into your SAP environment, now is the time. Whether you're looking to automate processes, improve decision-making, or simply enhance user experiences, SAP S/4HANA is ready to help you unlock the full potential of this transformative technology.

entry.title: How to restrict access to material types and material groups in SAP S4 HANA
entry.content:
Restricting access to material types and material groups in SAP S4 HANA 

In S4 HANA Procurement, material types and material groups are restricted using authorization objects M_MATE_MAR (Material Master: Material Types) and M_MATE_WGR (Material Master: Material Groups). Both these objects have a common field value called BEGRU (Authorization group).

Material types and material groups cannot be directly maintained in BEGRU. First, use the Tcode SE54 to create authorization groups. The authorization groups must then be mapped to material types and groups using the T-codes OMS2 and OMSF. Then these authorization groups should be maintained in a role.

Note: Material types with no authorization group will skip the M_MATE_MAR authority check. material groups with no authorization group will skip M_MATE_WGR. Hence, authorization groups should be maintained against all the material types (allowed and restricted) and material groups (allowed and restricted) to make the access restrictions work.

 

Authorization object

Description

Field1

Description

Field2

Description

M_MATE_MAR

(Material Master: Material Types)

Actvt

Activity

BEGRU

Authorization group

M_MATE_WGR

(Material Master: Material Groups)

Actvt

Activity

BEGRU

Authorization group

Let us look at an example of restricting users' access to specific material types and groups while using the T-codes MM01, MM02, and MM03.

Material types allowed for users – Goods. All the other material types other than Goods should be restricted for users.

Material groups allowed for users - Finance Services. All the other material groups other than Finance Services should be restricted for users.

Two sets of authorization groups should be created in SE54 for the case described above.

Z001 for allowed material types.

Z002 for restricted material types.

Z003 for allowed material groups.

Z004 for restricted material groups.

Steps:

  • Create authorization groups for material types and material groups using the Tcode SE54.

  • Map authorization groups with the material types using the Tcode OMS2 (maintain the authorization group Z001 against the allowed material types (Goods) and maintain the authorization group Z002 against all the restricted material types.)

  • Map authorization groups with the material groups using the T-code OMSF (maintain the authorization group Z003 against all the allowed material groups (Finance Services) and maintain the authorization group Z004 against all the restricted material groups.)

  • Maintain the authorization groups in the authorization objects (M_MATE_MAR and M_MATE_WGR) in PFCG.

entry.title: Hey! Have you heard of SAP University Alliances// SAP Next-Gen?
entry.content:
Become a member of the SAP University Alliances program and enhance your student's academic outcome. Collaborate on research projects and get access to SAP software, teaching materials and related support.

Dear Beginner Corner visitor, 

Either you are a student, professor, lecturer or are interested in learning about SAP, have you visited our SAP University Alliances webpage? 

About SAP University Alliances

The SAP University Alliances program enables academia to educate the next generation for the Intelligent Enterprise and the experience economy, engage at SAP events, build industry partnerships, and prepare graduates for the SAP ecosystem.

  • Teach hands-on with SAP technology at your university 

  • Participate in strategic research activities to build high-impact collaborations to educate the leaders of tomorrow

  • Foster close partnerships with the academic community through regional academic boards and educational institutions worldwide

In case you want to stayed tuned, check out for news in our SAP University Alliances community - public group for Latin America & Caribbean, Spain and Portugal where share events for academics, for students, free software access for academic usage, and further projects and initiatives. 

See you there! 

Best regards,

SAP University Alliances - LAC, Spain and Portugal

entry.title: Service Confirmation - Using Defined Logistics Scenario for Material Withdrawal
entry.content:
This blog will explore service confirmations using defined logistics scenarios for material withdrawal. There are primarily two types of withdrawals: one using Plant / Storage Location and another using Technician Consignment. Let's start with the example of withdrawal using a Plant / Storage Location

In a service scenario, suppose a piece of equipment needs to be serviced, and a service order is created. Once the service order is released for further processing, you can proceed with the following steps. Based on the configuration mentioned below, a reservation will be generated.

Path SPRO-> Service-> Transactions-> Setting for Service Transactions-> Integration-> Logistics Integration-> Organizational Management Legacy-> Define Logistics Scenario for Procurement of Materials and Services.

In this step, enter the required details such as the transaction type and item category, and select the logistics scenario as "Reservation."

Let's consider a spare part with a stock quantity of 30.

Once a service order is created with a spare part quantity of 4, release the service order. A reservation will then be generated for the 4 quantities.

In the transaction history, the reservation number will be displayed once it is successfully generated.

In the MMBE (Stock Overview) screen, you can view the same reservation details, including the reserved quantity and stock availability.

Next, you will create a follow-up and initiate the service confirmation.

 

Service confirmation are particularly useful because they allow you to confirm the use of spare parts. For instance, if the service order reserved four spare parts but only three were actually used, you can adjust the quantity in the service confirmation to reflect the usage of only three. After updating the quantity, save the service confirmation. Once saved, change the status to "Completed" to generate the material document based on below mentioned config.

Path SPRO-> Service-> Transactions-> Setting for Service Transactions-> Integration-> Logistics Integration-> Organizational Management Legacy-> Define Logistics Scenario for Material Withdrawal.

In this step, enter the required details such as the transaction type and item category, and select the logistics scenario as "P Plant / Storage Location."

Here I mentioned only 3 quantities.

The system will automatically issue the goods based on the reservation with the quantity specified in the service confirmation. In the transaction history, the Goods movement number will be displayed once it is successfully generated.

Open the document in MIGO.

Automatic goods issue happened only for quantity mentioned in service confirmation.

In the MMBE (Stock Overview) screen, you can view the same, including the reserved quantity and stock availability.

From 30 it is reduced to 27 in unrestricted stock; and from 4 it is reduced to 1 in reserved stock. Any remaining quantity can be returned to unrestricted stock using the MIGO transaction.

Return delivery against Reservation.

In the MMBE (Stock Overview) screen, you can view the updated details, including the reserved quantity and stock availability. After processing, the reserved quantity will be reduced to zero, and the unrestricted stock will increase to 28 (reflecting the return of 1 qty  from the reserved stock).

 

Now, Let's examine material withdrawal using Technician - Consignment. In this case, if equipment that needs servicing is installed in the field, the technician must travel to that location to perform the service. This scenario is often referred to as field service. To manage this, the technician will need to bring along some spare parts, and this withdrawal method helps track those parts.

Before proceeding, ensure the following prerequisites are met:

  1. Extend the technician  to Customer role in the Business Partner (BP) transaction.
  2. For the extended customer (Technician), create a consignment order using the VA01 transaction code with the order type KB (Consignment Fill-up).

Now, if you check, you'll see that there are 6 QTY available in the customer consignment stock.

In the above image, this stock is allocated to the Technician who will be going to the field to perform the service.

    3. Since we are withdrawing the material using consignment, reservations are not needed. Therefore, remove the relevant configuration.

Once all the prerequisites are completed, create a service order with a quantity of 5 and release the service order.

In the transaction history, you will see that no reservations have been generated.

 

Now, you need to create service confirmations to confirm the quantity. Since the technician went to the field with 6 quantities, the goods movement should be processed against those 6 quantities (from the consignment stock). For the automatic goods issue, the following configuraton needs to be done:

Path SPRO-> Service-> Transactions-> Setting for Service Transactions-> Integration-> Logistics Integration-> Organizational Management Legacy-> Define Logistics Scenario for Material Withdrawal.

In this step, enter the required details such as the transaction type and item category, and select the logistics scenario as "T Technician - Consignment."  with special stock indicator ‘W’.

Now, create the service confirmation and change the status to "Completed." This will finalize the service confirmation and trigger the necessary goods movement.

In the transaction history, the Goods movement number will be displayed once it is successfully generated.

Open the document in MIGO transaction to view the consignment issue against the technician, reflecting the material withdrawal from the consignment stock.

In the MMBE (Stock Overview) screen, you can view the same, including the Consignment stock and stock availability.

 

 

 

 

 

 

In this way, service confirmation plays a crucial role in automating material withdrawal from inventory. Thank you for taking the time to read the blog—I hope you found it helpful and informative!

SAP S/4HANA, SAP Field Service Management,  SAP S/4HANA Finance, SAP CRM Service Manager,  SAP CRM on-demand solution

entry.title: Quality Management with SAP Batch Release Hub and SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
In the ever-evolving pharmaceutical industry, the need for efficient and compliant batch release processes is key. Roche, a global leader in healthcare and pharmaceuticals, has taken significant strides in enhancing their quality management systems by leveraging SAP Batch Release Hub and SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The approach not only streamlines operations but also ensures adherence to stringent regulatory standards.

This was the topic of the episode 109 of the Podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud: Episode 109: Roche’s Quality Management Strategy with SAP Batch Release Hub and SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Check more details below!

 

The Power of Collaboration: Roche and SAP Co-Innovation

The journey of transforming Roche's batch release process began with a vision to integrate and automate quality management using SAP’s cutting-edge technology. The collaboration has the participation of Melanie Fabian and Dr. Marco Strohmeier from Roche, along with Aladdin Mandishah from SAP, who brought a wealth of industry knowledge and technical expertise to the table. They are the guests of the Episode 109, hosted by Markus Oertelt, product expert at SAP.

- Melanie Fabian: With a background in pharmacy and experience in pharmaceutical manufacturing and quality management, Melanie Fabian helped in defining the technical requirements and ensuring the system met regulatory expectations.

- Dr. Marco Strohmeier: Strohmeier's expertise in biotech manufacturing and quality, combined with process optimization, helped shaping the project’s direction towards a fully integrated quality management system.

- Aladdin Mandishah: As the director of product marketing in SAP’s life sciences and healthcare unit, Mandishah focused on aligning the solution with industry needs and promoting the tool within the sector.

 

Challenges and Innovations in Batch Release and Quality Management

The collaboration aimed to address several challenges inherent in the traditional batch release processes, such as data integration from multiple systems, compliance with global standards, and the need for automation to reduce manual checks.

Integration with Multiple Systems: Roche required a solution that could consolidate data from various external systems essential for batch release, such as quality control, deviation management, and regulatory compliance systems.

Automation and Compliance: The SAP Batch Release Hub was designed to automate critical checks and balances, reducing the reliance on manual processes and ensuring compliance with international standards.

 

The Outcome: A Flexible and Robust System

The co-innovation resulted in the creation of the SAP Batch Release Hub, a flexible tool tailored to meet the diverse needs of Roche’s production processes. This system enables Roche to manage different types of material releases through customizable workflows, ensuring efficiency and compliance across the board.

Material-Specific Workflows: The tool accommodates various release processes, from drug substances to production components, by allowing customization according to material type.

Adaptability to Business Needs: The SAP Batch Release Hub is designed to adapt to different business requirements and regulatory environments, ensuring that it can be deployed globally while meeting local compliance demands.

Learn more about SAP Batch Release Hub

 

Looking Ahead: Artificial Intelligence in Quality Management

While the current focus is on streamlining and integrating batch release processes, future enhancements could include the application of artificial intelligence (AI). AI has the potential to further improve efficiency by automating data interpretation and decision-making processes, particularly in areas like deviation management and supply chain optimization.

Check the GenAI-assisted Batch Release - Phase II at SAP Road Map Explorer

 

Tune into the Innovation Journey

To gain deeper insights into how Roche and SAP are pioneering advancements in pharmaceutical quality management, listen to the full episode of the podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Discover firsthand accounts from Melanie Fabian, Dr. Marco Strohmeier, and Aladdin Mandishah on the challenges, innovations, and successes of this project. This episode not only sheds light on the technical aspects of the SAP Batch Release Hub but also explores the collaborative efforts that made this innovation possible.

Listen to the Episode Now and explore how cutting-edge technology is shaping the future of pharmaceutical manufacturing and quality assurance.

 

Podcast Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud product insights, implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Check all the episodes at sap.com/podcasts, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, TuneIn, and more audio platforms.

entry.title: How to setup SAP CDC Solution in Azure Data Factory
entry.content:
Introduction

Azure Data Factory (ADF) is a data integration (ETL/ELT) Platform as a Service (PaaS) and for SAP data integration, ADF currently offers six connectors:

These connectors can only extract data in batches, where each batch treats old and new data equally w/o identifying data changes (“batch mode”).  This extraction mode isn’t optimal when dealing w/ large data sets, such as tables w/ millions/billions of records, that change often 

Consequently, customers have been asking for a new connector that can extract only data changes (inserts/updates/deletes = “deltas”), leveraging Change Data Capture (CDC) feature that exists in most SAP systems (“CDC mode”).  After gathering their requirements, New SAP CDC connector leveraging SAP Operational Data Provisioning (ODP) framework.  This new connector can connect to all SAP systems that support ODP, such as R/3, ECC, S/4HANA, BW, and BW/4HANA, directly at the application layer or indirectly via SAP Landscape Transformation (SLT) replication server as a proxy.  It can fully/incrementally extract SAP data that includes not only physical tables, but also logical objects created on top of those tables, such as ABAP) Core Data Services (CDS) views,

Architecture

The high-level architecture of our SAP CDC solution in ADF is divided into two sides, left-hand-side (LHS) and right-hand-side (RHS).  LHS includes SAP CDC connector that invokes ODP API over standard Remote Function Call (RFC) modules to extract raw SAP data (full + deltas).  RHS includes ADF copy activity that loads the raw SAP data into any destination, such as Azure Blob Storage/Azure Data Lake Store (ADLS) Gen2, in CSV/Parquet format, essentially archiving/preserving all historical changes.  RHS can also include ADF data flow activity that transforms the raw SAP data, merges all changes, and loads the result into any destination, such as Azure SQL Database/Azure Synapse Analytics, essentially replicating SAP data.  ADF data flow activity can also load the result into ADLS Gen2 in Delta format, enabling time-travel to produce snapshots of SAP data at any given periods in the past.  LHS and RHS can be combined as SAP CDC/replication template to auto-generate ADF pipeline that can be frequently run using ADF tumbling window trigger to replicate SAP data into Azure w/ low latency and w/o watermarking.

 

ADF copy activity w/ SAP CDC connector runs on a self-hosted integration runtime (SHIR) that you install on your on-premises/virtual machine, so it has a line of sight to your SAP source systems/SLT replication server, while ADF data flow activity runs on a serverless Databricks/Spark cluster, Azure IR.  SAP CDC connector via ODP can extract various data source (“provider”) types, such as:

 

  • SAP extractors, originally built to extract data from SAP ECC and load it into SAP BW
  • ABAP CDS views, the new data extraction standard for SAP S/4HANA
  • Info Providers and Info Objects in SAP BW and BW/4HANA
  • SAP application tables, when using SLT replication server as a proxy

 

These providers run on SAP systems to produce full/incremental data in Operational Delta Queue (ODQ) that is consumed by ADF copy activity w/ SAP CDC connector in ADF pipeline (“subscriber”).

INITIAL SLT CONFIGURATION

SLT is a database trigger-enabled CDC solution that can replicate SAP application tables and simple views in near real time. SLT replicates from SAP source systems to various targets, including the operational delta queue (ODQ). In S/4HANA 2020 and higher, the addon is already built into the S4CORE component.

During the replication process, database triggers track all changes to the data stored in the source tables. Every operation is registered in the logging table, and the function module transfers data to a specified target. SLT automatically creates all required objects when you initiate the data extraction. Keeping changes in the logging tables provides a level of fault tolerance that prevents data loss when the system that manages the replication is temporarily unavailable. In such a case, once the replication process is re-established, the SLT can easily identify all not-replicated changes and continue operation.


To start the replication process you need to set-up SAP SLT. You do it by creating configurations that controls the replication process. You manage the configuration in the SLT Cockpit (t-code: LTRC). In the Standalone deployment you should start the cockpit on the Replication Server, and not on the source.

The initial screen of the SLT Cockpit lists all previously created configurations. Each configuration has a unique identifier called Mass Transfer ID (MTID). Click the Create Configuration button to define a new one. It opens a wizard that guides you through basic settings.

The SAP SLT framework is part of the DMIS (Data Migration Server) component. In standalone deployment, you must install it on both systems: the source (transactional) system, as well as the one

The number of Data Transfer / Initial Load / Calculation Jobs should come from the sizing you perform. For small configuration, with limited number of tables in scope SAP recommends using not less than two data load jobs. There is 1-to-1 correlation between specified data transfer jobs and the dialog work processes in the source system.

The number of Initial Load Jobs should be lower than total number of data transfer jobs, otherwise after the initial load the system won’t replicate changes. Calculation Jobs run the initial assessment of the data stored in the source table and chunk data into smaller pieces.

Validation

To validate your SAP system configurations for ODP, you can run RODPS_REPL_TEST program to test the extraction of your SAPI extractors, CDS views, BW objects, etc.

Prepare SHIR (self-hosted integration runtime) w/ SAP CDC connector

To prepare SHIR w/ SAP CDC connector, complete the following steps: \

  1. On ADF Studio, create and configure SHIR, We can download our latest private SHIR version w/ improved performance and detailed error messages from https://adfsapfileshare.blob.core.windows.net/shir/IntegrationRuntime_5.18.8172.1.msi and install it on your on-premises/virtual machine.
  2. The more CPU cores you have on your SHIR machine, the higher your data extraction throughput.
  3. To avoid being replaced by later versions, on ADF Studio, go to the Integration runtimes section of Manage hub, select your SHIR to edit, and select the Auto update tab to disable this feature.

Download the latest 64-bit SAP .NET Connector (SAP NCo 3.0) from https://support.sap.com/en/product/connectors/msnet.html and install it on your SHIR machine.  During installation, select the Install Assemblies to GAC option in the Optional setup steps window. 

 

Add a network security rule on your SAP systems, so SHIR machine can connect to them.  If your SAP system is on Azure virtual machine (VM), add the rule by setting the Source IP addresses/CIDR ranges property to your SHIR machine IP address and the Destination port ranges property to 3200,3300.  For example:

 

On SHIR machine, run the following PowerShell cmdlet to ensure that it can connect to your SAP systems: Test-NetConnection <SAP system IP address> -port 3300 

 

Prepare SAP CDC linked service

To prepare SAP CDC linked service, complete the following steps:

On ADF Studio, go to the Linked services section of Manage hub and select the New button to create a new linked service.

 

  1. Search for SAP and select SAP CDC (Preview).

 

 

  1. Set SAP CDC linked service properties, many of them are similar to SAP Table linked service properties
    1. For the Connect via integration runtime property, select your SHIR.
    2. For the Server name property, enter the mapped server name for your SAP system.
    3. For the Subscriber name property, enter a unique name to register and identify this ADF connection as a subscriber that consumes data packages produced in ODQ by your SAP system. 

 

  1. Test the connection and create your new SAP CDC linked service.

Monitor data extractions on SAP systems

To monitor data extractions on SAP systems, complete the following steps:

 

  1. Using SAP Logon Tool on your SAP source system, run ODQMON transaction code.

 

 

 

  1. Enter the value for Subcriber name property of your SAP CDC linked service in the Subscriber input field and select All in the Request Selection dropdown menu to show all data extractions using that linked service.

 

  1. You can now see all registered subscriber processes in ODQ representing data extractions from ADF copy activities that use your SAP CDC linked service.  On each ODQ subscription, you can drill down to see individual full/delta extractions.  On each extraction, you can drill down to see individual data packages that were consumed.
  2. When ADF copy activities that extract SAP data are no longer needed, their ODQ subscriptions should be deleted, so SAP systems can stop tracking their subscription states and remove the unconsumed data packages from ODQ.  To do so, select the unneeded ODQ subscriptions and delete them.

 

Conclusion:

The SAP CDC connector in Data Factory reads delta changes from the SAP ODP framework. The deltas are recorded in ODQ tables.

Reference:

Overview and architecture of the SAP CDC capabilities - Azure Data Factory | Microsoft Learn

entry.title: Discovering Business Events from Lobby for SAP Build Process Automation : Manage Credit Memo Request
entry.content:
Introduction....

SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) enables business users to automate workflow processes and tasks without writing code.

Check here for What's New and Learn more from the roadmap.  

This August 2024, SAP Build Process Automation has a released a new way of discovering Business Events from Lobby.

It enables users to discover events from Business Accelerator hub and to view the detailed event catalogue. It also facilitates reuse by creating centrally managed Events projects at Lobby level. Additionally using the Manual Event Specification Upload feature, all the SAP and non-SAP business events supporting CloudEvent1.0 format are supported which can be configured with Event Mesh(or Advanced Event Mesh) at runtime.

This blog will demonstrate the latest features of event triggers in the context of a SAP S/4HANA business use case:

  1. Monitoring Business Events tile
  2. Creating Event Projects from Lobby and consuming the events as event triggers in SAP Build Process Automation
  3. Run Now feature

The business use case explained….

Credit Memo Requests in SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 

A credit memo request is a sales document used in complaints processing to request credit for a customer. Creating credit memo requests is a part of Sales and Distribution in SAP S/4HANA.  

In situations where the calculated prices are incorrect, or if discounts are not applied etc. a credit memo request can be created. The credit memo request can be automatically blocked for credit checking. Once it has been approved, you can remove the block. 

The system uses the credit memo request to create a credit memo. 

The SBPA Process for Manage Credit Memo Requests enables the business user to view the Credit Memo Request which are configured for external approvals and make a decision to Approve or Reject the Credit Memo Request which in turn will Release or Reject it in SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

 

Before we begin….

  • Credit Memo Requests are created in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud 
  • Credit Memo Request lifecycle events are pushed from SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to Business Technology Platform through the Event Mesh configuration. Follow the blog  Event Mesh and SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Setup for SAP Build Process Automation for details on the setup to be done.

 

Creating Event Projects From Lobby….

From the SAP Build Lobby, click on Events and click Create . 

 

There are two options - One to browse SAP Business Accelerator Hub to discover public business events or to upload an event specification file. Choose SAP Business Accelerator Hub for this particular use case.

Search with Credit Memo and Select Credit Memo Request Events SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for Business Events

All the available Credit Memo Request Events are displayed with relevant details. Click Add.

The Project Name and Description is automatically populated. Click Create to create the event project.

The Event Project is now created. Click Release to release the project.

 

Choose the required option and click Release.

The Event Project is now released. Click Publish to publish the project to Library. Once the event project is released to Library it can be consumed in SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA).

Click Publish.

Now the Event Project is ready to be consumed in the SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA) process.

Adding the event trigger….

The events available in the events project released in the above step are available as a part of the Library and can be used in the SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA).

For example, when you add an Event Trigger to the process , it automatically shows the list of events available.

In the SAP Build Process Automation(SBPA) process, click on Add a Trigger.

Click on Wait for an Event.

Select CreditMemoRequest Created event and click on Add.

 

Note that all the required details are filled, click on Create.

 

The event trigger is added to the process.

 

Designing the process….

A sample business process for Manage Credit Memo Requests could look like the following:

 

 

  • An event trigger is used to start the business process. The event used here is CreditMemoRequestCreated Event.
  • A further API Call is made to read the header information of the Credit Memo Request. The Credit Memo Request ID from the previous step is used as input for this API Call. The API in this example is
  • GET /A_CreditMemoRequest('{CreditMemoRequest}')
  • A set of business rules define the approver for the Credit Memo Request approval. The output is an approver email ID.
  • The approver determined from the previous step gets an Approval Task to release the Credit Memo Request in My Inbox with details of header and items and an option to Approve or Reject the request.
  • If the approver approves the credit memo request, quotes are added to the Credit Memo Request ID and the Credit Memo Request is released for further processing in the SAP S/4HANA system. The approval status will show ‘Released’ as the status of the Credit Memo Request.  An approval notification is sent to the stakeholders.
  • The API used in this example is POST /releaseApprovalRequest
  • If the approver rejects the credit memo request, quotes are added to the Credit Memo Request ID and the Credit Memo Request Approval Status is updated as Rejected in SAP S/4HANA system. A rejection notification is sent to the stakeholders.
  • The API used in this example is POST /rejectApprovalRequest

Now our SBPA process is ready.

Expert Tip :  Ensure to test your Actions projects and Events setup before testing 
the process to ensure they are working as expected. 
This will save time for troubleshooting and debugging later.

 

End-to-end execution….

  • When a credit memo request is created in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud and is configured for external approval, then it automatically triggers the defined process in SAP Build Process Automation.

 

  • Just for confirmation, check the Approval Status in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

 

  • Check if you received the event in Monitoring -> Acquired Events -> Business Events Tile. The events are persisted for 7 days and can be downloaded. Also they are very helpful in troubleshooting to understand if the events are correctly being received by SAP Build Process Automation.

 

 

  • You should get a task in My Inbox to Approve the Credit Memo Request

 

Add some comments and click approve.

When a Credit Memo Request is approved, it is released for further processing in the S/4HANA Public Cloud. The approval status will show ‘Released’ as the status of the Credit Memo Request   

 

OR  Add some comments and click Reject. Check that the S/4HANA Approval Status is updated as Rejected.

You can also check the SBPA Logs to check the details.

 

Run Now….

You can now test your processes locally that use event triggers by simulating the event trigger payload without connecting to the system or having an event mesh setup. This is to test if your end-to-end process works fine with the expected payload format for

“Run Now” action for event triggers in Trigger view allows to execute the event trigger in order to initiate event delivery with the user-provided data immediately. For more information, please refer to documentation here.

Below is  sample screenshot from Control Tower-> Environments -> Select your environment -> Triggers tab -> Select the required Event Trigger -> Run Now.

Enter a valid payload in the Run Now UI and Click Run Now.

 

The process is triggered with the sample payload.

 Note: The Manage Credit Memo Requests SBPA Process neither replaces nor has all the features of SAP Credit Management or Financial Supply Chain Management. It enables an approval process with information about the credit memo request. Our customers could use and extend this process for exception management, notifications, auditing for the particular credit memo requests after approval from one or more personas like credit manager, finance analysts, etc. 

Other use cases….

There are some additional use cases for business event triggers:

Business Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud : The Customer Return ...

Business Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA: Manage Credit Block on Sale...

Business Event Triggers in SAP Build Process Automation for SAP S/4HANA: Manage Billing Block on Sal...

Do write to me in case of blockers, queries and feedback.

entry.title: DMOVE2S4 – your one-step approach into the cloud
entry.content:
Highlights

DMOVE2S4 is a one-step solution provided by Software Update Manager that integrates the transition to the cloud with system maintenance. It supports products that are built on SAP S/4HANA and requires a strong and stable network for data transfer. 

 

Introducing DMOVE2S4

With Software Update Manager 2.0, SAP offers the swiss army knife for maintenance scenarios. It supports operating system and database migrations, software updates and upgrades, system conversions to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition, as well as downtime-optimization scenarios for these tasks. 

Database Migration Option to SAP S/4HANA (on hyperscaler), short DMOVE2S4, is an offering by Software Update Manager for the seamless transition to RISE with SAP, combining the move from on-premise to the cloud with either a system conversion from SAP ERP 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA or an update/upgrade of SAP S/4HANA as the following picture illustrates.

 

And the best part? DMOVE2S4 is built on the proven mechanisms of Software Update Manager, ensuring a smooth and reliable journey into the cloud. Those who are familiar with DMO know, that there are two approaches which differ (not only) in their means of data transfer: 

  • DMO – transfers data based on memory pipes 
  • DMO with System Move (⚠️ not to be confused with DMOVE2S4) – transfers data based on data files 

For DMOVE2S4, the approach Database Migration Option (DMO) with system maintenance (either system conversion or upgrade) is combined with the move to a hyperscaler. Sounds simple but offers amazing results: You can do your move to the cloud in one step with the system maintenance and are ready to go. This also means, that DMOVE2S4 is not a separate selection in the Software Update Manager User Interface. On top, DMOVE2S4 can be combined with downtime-optimized DMO as well as downtime-optimized Conversion. It is worth mentioning that the DMOVE2S4 approach can also be used for moving to the cloud even without updating or upgrading your SAP S/4HANA system, although other transfer methods such as tenant copy of SAP HANA may be more suitable in such cases.

To benefit from DMOVE2S4 following prerequisites must be considered: 

  • Only target products based on SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Foundation are supported. 
  • The Network connection must be sufficient*.

*Please refer to the latest DMO Guide for details.

If the prerequisites cannot be met, Software Update Manager also got your back with DMO with System Move. The approach also allows for a smooth transition to the cloud; however, it cannot be combined with downtime-optimization approaches such as downtime-optimized DMO or downtime-optimized Conversion. 

For further information please refer to the following links: 

DMO Guide: DMO Move to SAP S/4HANA (on Hyperscaler) | SAP Help Portal 

SAP Note: 3431829 - Database Migration Option (DMO) of SUM 2.0 SP20, section g) 

SAP HANA Guide: Copying and Moving Tenant Databases (without software change)

 

Questions? Feedback? Concerns? Comment below and let us know!

entry.title: BRIM - SAP S/4 HANA Service: Subscription Order Management – Process Flow
entry.content:
In this blog, I would like to provide information on the master data and configuration steps required to implement the SOM process.

1. Overview of SOM Process from SAP

The Subscription Order Management component of SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) helps explore new B2C and B2B markets or expand market opportunities by targeting new customer segments across various verticals and geographies. SOM also provides master data, such as business partners, contract accounts, and products. Changes to existing contracts are managed within the SOM system. SAP Subscription and Order Management is the application that manages the sales process within the SAP Billing and Revenue Innovation Management (BRIM) solution. Subscription order management is part of SAP S/4 HANA Service.

2. Master Data: Business Partner and Contract Account

In SAP S/4HANA, business partners are central objects with different roles based on business needs. A business partner is used in the roles Sold-to-Party / Contract Partner. A business partner role, for example, contact, sold-to party or supplier, is used to classify a business partner in business function. 

For SOM in SAP BRIM, the MKK role is needed for integration with FI-CA, and these partners are replicated to SAP Convergent Charging as subscriber accounts. If SD processes are involved, the Sold-To role is required, and the SD customer is automatically created through customer-vendor integration. Business partners represent all parties involved in transactions, such as customers, debtors, creditors, or suppliers.

a) Business Partner Role

b) Business Partner Role Category

c) Create Business Partner

You can check config setting in SPRO – Service – Master Data – Business Partner

d) Extend Business Partner FLCU01 Customer

e) Extend BP to MKK Contract Partner

Using default Credit Profile Risk Class and Biller Direct are integrated with FSCM so we don’t use for SOM.

3.Contract Account

In SAP S/4HANA, only the contract account is used both in FI-CA and in Subscription Order Management (SOM). A business partner can be assigned to more than one contract account. For each item in a subscription order / contract a different contract account related to the business partner can be assigned.

a) Contract Account – Transaction code /nCAA1

b) Configure Contract Account Categories /OFICAIMG

Category Extended FI-CA recommended by SAP. If using BRIM for telecommunication then use category T Telecommunication, for Utility use R Utility Companies.

Select One Business Partner if using for only one business partner and if you uncheck then it will use for multiple contracts.

Select collective bill account if using and it is consolidation of billing.

c. Create Contract Account – Transaction code /nCAA1

All the values will be discussed with finance before maintaining including Payment Terms. The subscription contract created for the IT department from SAP SOM to FI-CA of SAP S/4HANA and to SAP CC.

4. Creation of Subscription Product: Subscription products that are used in subscription orders

You can check config setting in SPRO – Service – Master Data – Setting for Subscription Master Data

a. Material Type

b. Product Purpose Product Type – SPRO - Service

c. Product Master from S/4

d. Billing cycle

Billing plan holds the information about the billing cycle, reference to the provider contract item, payment amount, and payment currency.

Billing Cycle -If it is monthly default then select here else you can select in sales order

Select billing cycle determination rule and contract renewal

5. Customization of Subscription order:

a. Define Transaction Type:

SPRO – Service – Transactions - Basic Settings. This transaction types used to create subscription order

Order and Contract Types: PRVC – Subscription Contract and PRVO – Subscription Order

b. Subscription order

c. Subscription order settings: Main settings for Subscription Order Management (SOM) can be found under SPRO - Service - Master Data - Settings for Subscription Master Data and SPRO – Service - Transactions - Settings for Subscription Transactions

d. Contract assignment for the subscription order is mandatory with this setting

Item Categories for subscription order can be found under SPRO – Service – Basis Settings

e. Subscription Specific Customizing for Item Categories

6. Create Subscription order: Selection of the transaction type PRVO subscription order.

A subscription order is used to sell products and services by creating a subscription contract, including usage-based, recurring, and one-time charges.

a. Select Sold-to Party

b. Select product and contract

If having multiple contact account, then select from Assign Contract Account selection

c. Select Organization data

d. Select Technical Resources: For example, in the telecommunication industry, technical data like mobile numbers or SIM card numbers are entered, and each contract has a unique technical resource.

e. Pricing

f. Submit Order and Subscription order is created

g. It will create provider contract, and you can see below subscription document number

h. You can see below provider contract number

When a subscription order with a recurring fee is submitted, a subscription contract is created. During contract distribution to Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable (FI-CA), the contract items with recurring fees in the pricing elements are identified.

7. Subscription order with sales order for the finished product

a) Sales order 80789

b) Document flow with delivery and invoice

c)  Invoicing from CI

8. Subscription order with sales and Subscription item

a) Recurring product with contract validity

b) Subscription order for services

c) Contract

d) Provider Contract FP_VT3

e)  Transaction FKKBIXBIT_MON – to check contract billable item

You can display and analyze billable items based on selection criteria, regardless of their processing status. To run the BIT monitor, go to the SAP Easy Access menu and choose: Accounting - Financial Accounting - Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable -Convergent Invoicing - Billable Items - Display (Transaction FKKBIXBIT_MON). You can select individual billable items or use predefined selection variants for different processing statuses.

Thank you for reading this article!

entry.title: Help! Why Don’t I Get a Notification for My Custom Situation?
entry.content:
You’ve configured your own situation use case using the Extended Framework for Situation Handling. Now you’re waiting for a notification but nothing happens. What could have gone wrong?

Let me ask you a few questions to check whether your configuration is correct.

  • Has a situation occurred?
  • How have the conditions been set up?
  • Are all situation triggers covered?
  • Is recipient determination working?
  • Have email notifications been set up correctly?

These questions can support you as an SAP S/4HANA Cloud or SAP S/4HANA customer.

 

Has a situation occurred?

This may seem trivial but depending on the conditions that have been defined for the situation type, a situation instance may not have been created. An easy way to find out is for the user is to check the My Situations – Extended app to see if there’s an instance that they should have been notified about. If the instance is not displayed in the app, continue checking the conditions. If there’s an instance but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle settings.

List of situaitons in My Situations – Extended app

Or, as a key user, you can display the current situation instances for a situation type by choosing Manage Instances in the header of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app.

Manage Instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

In the list of situation instances you can see if a notification was sent and when. Depending on the configuration, a notification can be sent only when a situation occurs for the first time or each time the situation is updated. If you don’t see an entry here, no notification was sent.

List of situation instances in Manage Situation Types - Extended app

There’s also the Monitor Situations – Extended app for key users that shows all created situation instances and all activities for an instance. You need to have situation monitoring enabled for the situation type in the Situation Instances section.

Enable the monitoring in Manage Situations - Extended app

List of situation instances in the Monitor Situations - Extended app

If no situation instance is displayed in the Manage Situation Types – Extended app and in the Monitor Situations – Extended app, this means that the users did not receive a notification because there no situation instance was created.  

If you expected a situation based on the conditions that you have defined, you may find clues in the following section on conditions.

If a situation instance is displayed but no notification was sent, check the notifications / life cycle setting.

How have the conditions been set up?

In the Conditions section of the Manage Situation Types – Extended app, let’s look at the settings for Notifications / Life Cycle, the Order of the conditions, and the Filters.

Notifications / life cycle settings

Each condition can have a different Notifications/Life Cycle setting. Make sure you’ve assigned the appropriate ones.

Notification setting

You can select from three standard options:

  1. The notification is sent to all recipients when a situation is created. When the situation is updated, only the assigned processors are notified.
  2. No one receives a notification after a situation is closed.
  3. All recipients receive a notification after a closed situation has been reopened (this option is available only for object-based situations with the instance closing behavior Close & Keep)

If you’ve defined your own Notification/Life Cycle behavior, I recommend double-checking the life cycle transitions. Make sure you have considered all relevant transitions from creation to closure. And check the notification settings for each transition.

Consider the processing order of conditions

If you’ve set up multiple conditions, the order of those conditions is important. The order defines the sequence in which the conditions are executed. It’s important to understand that the processing of the conditions stops when the first condition in the processing order is met. Conditions lower in the sequence are ignored. For this reason, you need to ensure that the more specific conditions, for which you want to have notifications, are being processed before the more generic conditions. Pay special attention to conditions that do not send notifications and those that close instances. If they are too high up in the sequence other conditions may always be ignored.

Processing order of conditions

Condition filters

Effective filters need to be of the same data format as the values in the CDS fields, such as strings or integers. You can find the reference to your object’s CDS views in the Manage Situation Objects app.

Sometimes the conditions and their filter values have been set up in such a way that no instances are created. Check whether one of the following reasons has prevented the creation of a situation instance:

  • The condition attribute filters are too constrained for your use case.
  • The attribute filter values overlap.
  • The attribute filter values are contradictory.

The condition and filter settings depend on your use case and the situation template on which your situation type is based. If you use multiple conditions, you can easily compare filters using the Compare Filters function.

Compare condition filters

Compare condition filters used

You can find some examples of using multiple conditions with filters in the blog posts Custom Situation Cases: Configure an Escalation Case and Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

 

Are all situation triggers covered?

Complex use cases often require multiple situation triggers. Check whether you have configured all possible triggers. And ensure that all triggers are enabled. There are two kinds of triggers: batch-based and event-based triggers.

Batch-based triggers periodically check the data you have defined in the condition filters.

  • The triggering object can be the anchor object or the trigger object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object is always Add/Update because the object still exists.

Use the Trigger Batch function to test immediately whether the situation trigger works as expected. Also check batch scheduling to make sure it’s configured correctly.

Batch-based situation triggers

Event-based triggers are based on an object’s status update. You can use multiple event-based triggers where you need to consider three aspects:

  • The triggering object: Multiple objects may impact a situation.
  • The trigger events such as create, update, and remove: Multiple events may impact a situation for a specific object.
  • The behavior of the trigger object can be Add/Update, Remove (Trigger data available), or Remove (Trigger data not available)

Event-based situation triggers

And these three aspects need to go hand in hand.

Situation objects that are created always have the trigger object behavior Add/Update because the object is added and exists.

The events that change an object can be manifold, for example, updated, confirmed, rejected, in progress, for approval, completed, released, posted, published, started, activated, blocked, unlocked, and many more. If the object still exists after the change, the trigger object behavior is Add/Update. If the object is flagged as removed or deleted, the trigger object behavior is Remove (Trigger data available). 

Objects that are deleted always have the trigger object behavior Remove (trigger data not available). This means the object is physically gone along with all its data. No more references are possible.

You can find an example for multiple triggers in the blog post Custom Situation Cases: Configure a Complex Case.

Should a situation trigger be missing in the situation type, you have the following options in the test system:

  1. Add a situation trigger from the value help in the situation type.
  2. If no appropriate situation trigger is available for the situation type, you can add a situation trigger with the suitable event to your situation scenario (Manage Situation Scenarios app). To do this you need to have the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (1).  Optionally, you can add the situation trigger to the related template so that it’s automatically available for all situation types created from the situation template.
  3. If no matching situation trigger is available in the situation scenario, you can enhance or add a situation object with the missing situation trigger (Manage Situation Objects app). Again, you need the extensibility specialist role. Then proceed with (2) and (1).

Afterwards, transport your situation type, situation scenario, and situation object to the productive system.

 

Is recipient determination working?

Situation Handling uses Responsibility Management to determine the users that are responsible for a situation. In the My Situations – Extended app, users see the created situations, and, depending on the notification settings, receive notifications.

When you create a situation type in the Recipients section, there are two basic options for defining these users:

  • Responsibility by Teams, which determines the users with the help of a team category.
  • Responsibility Rules, which determines the users with a BAdI business logic.

Situation recipients

Responsibility by Teams

To determine the right users by teams, a team category needs to be in place. The team category links Situation Handling and Responsibility Management. For a team category, at least one team needs to be enabled so that the right users can be detected. You enable teams in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app. Also check whether all enabled teams have team members assigned to them and include the users who have to be informed about the situation.

Situation recipients by teams

You can add team members by selecting users from the value help. If you can’t find the user in the value help, check whether this user is registered as a business partner. If the business partner registration is missing, contact your system administrator to have your users registered.

If you use filters for responsibility definition or member functions in your situation type, make sure they are appropriate and don’t exclude your users. For detailed information about these filters, see the blog post Using Responsibility Teams in Situation Handling.

Responsibility Rules

Responsibility rules use the logic of a BAdI to determine the right users. If the identification of users isn’t working as expected, the logic of the BAdI can be the reason why your users were not found and therefore did not get any notifications.

Situation recipients by rules

If your situation type uses a standard responsibility rule, please open a ticket on component CA-GTF-RM to have the rule checked.

For your custom implementation, you can check the BAdI implementation logic yourself. (You’ll need the SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC role to do that). Then proceed as follows:

  • In the Manage Responsibility Context app, on the Extended tab, search for the parent responsibility context to find your extended responsibility context.
  • Open the extended responsibility context to display the details. Here, you can see the responsibility rule ID associated with the responsibility rule that is displayed in the situation type.
  • In the Custom Logic app, you need to filter the list for the responsibility rule business context. Then, search for the static filter value that is identical to the responsibility rule ID. In the details, you can then check the BAdI implementation logic.

For detailed information about custom responsibility rules, you can refer to the blog post Responsibility Management: Create Your Rules. The blog post Using Responsibility Rules in Situation Handling illustrates how responsibility rules and Situation Handling work hand in hand.

 

Have email notifications been set up correctly?

In addition to the SAP Fiori launchpad notifications, users can opt to receive email notifications for each situation type in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. For details, see Enable Email Notifications for End Users.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2408 email notifications are enabled automatically. In older releases users need to enable email notifications in their SAP Fiori launchpad settings. To be able to activate this option, users need to have received an SAP Fiori launchpad notification before they can see and select the situation type in the launchpad settings.

Notification settings

Users may also want to check the launchpad settings to see whether the email address for their user account is correct:

Email settings

If all the settings are correct and you're still not getting any emails, you may want to check whether the email domain is on the list of allowed domains. The list of sender domains is maintained in Output Management. You can find more information in the chapter Defining an Email Sender Domain in the Setup Guide for SAP Best Practices for Output Management.

For specific questions or issues with the notification framework, please open a ticket on component OPU-GW-NOT.

 

Additional prerequisites for SAP S/4HANA

As an SAP S/4HANA customer, you may need to check some additional settings for the Notification Channel Framework which are automatically in place for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers. You can also refer to the blog post Enable Email Notification in Notification Channel Framework. If you want to enable email notifications per default, you can implement the SAP Note 3417113.

To be on the safe side, you may also want to check whether you have made the required technical configuration settings.

  • Set up technical jobs
  • Activate OData services for apps
  • Define authorization for apps (optional)
  • Activate OData services for APIs (optional)
  • Define authorizations for APIs (optional)

 

Did this help you?

If not → just open a ticket on our component CA-SIT-ENG and we’ll take care of your problem. In the ticket, please let us know the situation scenario and the situation template your situation type is based on. Please attach screenshots of your situation type’s Conditions and Recipients sections. Or tell us which of the steps above you’ve already tried.

 

For more information, see

SAP Help Portal for

  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP Community

  • Intelligent Situation Handling

entry.title: Search, Select, and Straighten
entry.content:
Advanced Variant Configuration (AVC) offers a wide variety of tools for managing configurable products. Characteristics and their values, describing the properties of a configurable material, make up the scaffold of configuration models – including a more or less complex net of dependencies between the characteristics and characteristic values.

Sometimes, the number of characteristics in a model can reach an enormous level, and you can quickly lose track of things: Which group does the characteristic belong to? Where in the BOM hierarchy is it located? It would be nice if you could search for characteristics in a vast model effectively, and even better if you could also directly valuate the characteristic you’ve been searching for. As you might have guessed, we’ve got good news: Searching and working within search results is now possible (since SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408).

This is how it works: On the valuation screen in the simulation, there’s a new search field.

Here, you can enter the technical name or description of a characteristic. Whether you can use the technical name or the description (or both) depends on the settings for the description mode:

You can adjust this setting to your ease.

The search is case-insensitive and, important to know, it comprises all characteristic groups and configurable components of the bill of materials (BOM). This way, you’re not limited to the selected characteristic group on the screen.

As you can see in the following example, searching for "length" returns results from different BOM positions in the model:

You can immediately assign (or remove) values to the displayed characteristics when you see the results list.

This new feature will make handling the characteristic values of even humongous models much easier!

Check this link to our documentation to watch a short video showing the search feature: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/f369b2eff700401494ba6e7c9a573288/11d7452297a74a499d56f5049daa3606.html?version=2408.500.

entry.title: Customer Course GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services) Available for SAP S/4HANA 2023
entry.content:
The GW100 got a “technical update”, which means that all exercises are now running on the newest SAP S/4HANA available today. In addition, the slides now follow the newest design guidelines of SAP. So, welcome to this short blog post about the customer training GW100 (SAP Gateway – Building OData Services).

If you are more interested in a digital learning for SAP Gateway, please read my blog post:
SAP Learning Journey – Building OData Services with SAP Gateway

Introduction

Starting in 2014, this is now the eighth incarnation of how to develop OData services with SAP Gateway. The course shows all parts of SAP Gateway, which are available since SAP NetWeaver Application Server 7.0 (SAP Gateway 2.0), as well as parts added up to SAP S/4HANA 2023. The whole bandwidth of SAP Gateway covering all releases is shown.

The units in the current version are the following:

  1. SAP Gateway Overview
  2. OData Overview
  3. SAP Gateway Service Implementation
  4. SAP Gateway Service Generation
  5. SAP Gateway Service Redefinition
  6. SAP Gateway and CDS Views
  7. SAP Gateway Hub Functionalities
  8. Advanced OData Options
  9. SAP Gateway Security
  10. Further Information

If customers are interested, they can order a customer specific version of the course including only those topics suitable for their release or purpose.

System Landscape

Let’s start with the system release: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02. Our system landscape – we call it universal target – is used in many technology courses and offers full access for the participants from the SAP Fiori launchpad (FLP) in the browser down to SAP HANA on the SUSE Linux Enterprise server (SLES). Everything is set up following the newest guidelines of SAP so that it can really act as a template for customers.

GW100 System Landscape (Screenshot from System Setup Guide)

In addition, we offer an instance of the SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) to show the consumption of OData services. If you want to know more about this topic, check out our courses for SAP Fiori development.

Example: Business Services

As an example from the material, here a shorten excerpt about defining business services.

Business Services: OData V2 and V4 (Screenshot from GW100)

In the area of business services, a service definition exposes CDS Views as a generic service. A service binding then marks it as a special kind of service. You can create an OData V2 or V4 service for API- or UI-usage. Service definitions and bindings are created in the ADT, the generated OData services are registered in transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE or /IWFND/V4_ADMIN respectively.

How to Create a Service Definition and Binding (Screenshot from GW100)

Service definitions and bindings are ABAP repository objects and a substantial part of the business services. They can only be created and edited in the ADT. In the first step, the service definition just defines the name of the service. The CDS views are added later in the source code.

In the second step, the service binding refers to the service definition and sets the binding type, which are mainly communication protocols.

Service Definition and Binding Correlation (Screenshot from GW100)

By exposing a CDS View in a service definition, the name of the entity set can be defined. For the entity type, "Type" is automatically appended to the entity set name.

The entity set names and navigation properties based on the associations in the CDS views are visible in the service binding on the right. For OData V2, multiple versions of a service definition can be added to a service binding visible on the left. For OData V4, multiple service definitions can be added to one service binding to allow cross-service navigation.


Summary

If you have read so far, it seems that you are really thinking about attending the course. Don’t hesitate and check out the physical and virtual events we offer:

https://training.sap.com/course/GW100

If you are more interested in a digital learning, please visit https://learning.sap.com. Some parts of the GW100 are already available free-of-charge:

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/building-odata-services-with-sap-gateway

For any other questions around the offerings of SAP Learning Services, please jump to our community page and get in contact with us:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/training-certification

I hope even if you won’t attend the GW100 in any kind, you found some useful information around SAP Gateway in this blog post.

Happy learning
Stefan

entry.title: Where can I start learning how to utilize the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit within SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Implementing SAP Self-Billing Cockpit learning journey.

Overview
This learning journey will focus on the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit, an SAP Industry Cloud solution designed to streamline and automate self-billing processes. The key functionalities such as receiving self-billing transmissions, updating invoices and journal entries, detecting and recording differences, and preparing documents for the clearing process in accounts receivable will be covered. You will learn to analyze and monitor usage, manage error lists, and understand the commercial model and licensing metrics. The course aims to equip SAP consultants with the knowledge to effectively implement and utilize the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit within SAP S/4HANA environments.

Learning objectives
At the end of this learning journey, participants will be able to effectively navigate and utilize the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit within SAP S/4HANA environments. Learners will gain proficiency in receiving self-billing transmissions, updating invoices and journal entries, and managing the clearing process in accounts receivable. Additionally, the course will cover how to detect and record discrepancies in self-billing documents and prepare them for confirmation. Participants will also learn to analyze and monitor usage, manage error lists, and understand the commercial model and licensing metrics associated with the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit. This comprehensive understanding will enable SAP consultants to implement and optimize the use of the SAP Self-Billing Cockpit, enhancing their ability to streamline and automate self-billing processes effectively.

Pass all the quizzes and receive a digital badge

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Changes in the Communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy
entry.content:
Versão em português

Hi everyone,

The SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, outbound invoicing option for Brazil team has released updates on the communication of the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution with SEFAZ.

NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version

This update complies with the SEFAZ NF3-e Technical Note 2024.002 1.01 Version, which announced the discontinuation of the asynchronous authorization service, also known as the batch submission service. The communication between the SAP service and SEFAZ now is synchronous.

This communication change has been updated in the outbound invoicing option for Brazil service, and it is not necessary to implement SAP Notes in your backend system. For official communication on this update, read the SAP Note 3509349.

Check out the latest updates and features in our What's New session. And for a step-by-step guide on how to use the Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy (NF3-e) solution, refer to the following SAP Help Portal pages:

  • Integration > NF3-e Specifics
  • Using Outbound Nota Fiscal > NF3-e Specifics

Related blog posts:

  • Usage Analytics Dashboard
  • How to Easily Browse the SAP Help Portal Pages for Outbound Nota Fiscal
  • An Introduction to SAP Document and Reporting Compliance: Navigating the SAP Help Portal

You can sign up for email alerts when new SAP Notes are released or updated in the components listed below; just follow the instructions in SAP Knowledge Base Article 2478289.

  • CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-BR-OU (Electronic Nota Fiscal for Electrical Energy, NF3-e)
  • FI-LOC-NFE-BR-OUT (Nota Fiscal Eletrônica Outbound)

Use the regulatory change manager tool to find out if a solution to a legal change is planned. Take a look at SAP Knowledge Base Article 3421995 and the regulatory change manager page to understand how to use this tool.

Have you found this blog post useful? If so, choose “like” (kudos), share it with your colleagues, and follow me for similar content. You are welcome to leave your questions and read similar blog posts on the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance SAP Community. Don’t forget to follow the SAP Electronic Invoicing for Brazil (SAP Nota Fiscal Eletronica) tag and the SAP Document and Reporting Compliance community to keep up with the latest NF-e news.

Thank you, and until next time,
Thiago
